_comment: Target pipeline architecture for Pre-IPO Compliance Gate. The exported JSON shows default state (Input → 1 AI Model → Output). This spec documents what needs to be built in the Airia visual editor.

target_nodes.notes: Receives PR webhook payload: { repo, pr_number, pr_url, sender }
Create new AI Operation node. Paste Agent 1 prompt. Set temp=0.3. Assign license_scanner MCP tool.
Create new AI Operation node. Paste Agent 2 prompt. Set temp=0.3. Enable chat history to receive Agent 1 output. Assign secrets_scanner MCP tool.
Repurpose existing 'AI Model' node. Rename to 'Compliance Report Generator'. Change temp from 1.0 → 0.7. Paste Agent 3 prompt. Assign pr_reporter MCP tool.
Add Memory node in Write mode. Enable shared access. Stores: PR number, scan date, verdict, finding counts, repo.
Reconnect from Memory Write (currently connected to the single AI Model node).

target_flow: Input → License Audit Agent → Secrets Scanner → Compliance Reporter → Memory Write → Output

license_scanner_lambda.description: Fetches PR diff + dependency manifests from GitHub, identifies licenses, returns structured findings

secrets_scanner_lambda.description: Fetches PR file contents from GitHub, pattern-matches secrets/financial data/PII

pr_reporter_lambda.description: Posts compliance report as PR comment, creates blocking issues, writes audit log to S3

build_steps: 2. Rename existing 'AI Model' node → 'Compliance Report Generator'
5. Add new AI Operation node: 'License Audit Agent' (temp=0.3, Agent 1 prompt)
6. Add new AI Operation node: 'Secrets & Financial Data Scanner' (temp=0.3, Agent 2 prompt)
8. Connect: Input → License Audit → Secrets Scanner → Compliance Reporter → Output
9. Add Memory (Write) node between Compliance Reporter and Output
11. Register 3 Lambda endpoints as MCP tools in Airia
14. Test with sample input from examples/demo_scenarios.md
